questions.question: What is the largest species of shark?
questions.additionalInfo: The Whale Shark is the largest species of shark and can grow up to 40 feet in length.

questions.question: What shark lives the longest?
questions.additionalInfo: The Greenland Shark is known to live for over 400 years, making it the longest-living vertebrate.

questions.question: How does the tiny Cookiecutter Shark get its name?
answers: It leaves cookie-shaped wounds on its prey
It looks like a chocolate chip cookie
questions.correctAnswer: It leaves cookie-shaped wounds on its prey
questions.additionalInfo: The Cookiecutter Shark gets its name from the cookie-shaped wounds it leaves on larger animals, which it feeds on.

questions.question: Which shark is able to 'walk' on land and survive out of water for up to 2 hours?
questions.additionalInfo: The Epaulette Shark is known for its ability to 'walk' on land using its pectoral fins and can survive out of water for up to 2 hours.

questions.question: The Bonnethead Shark is the only omnivorous shark. What does it eat that makes it omnivorous?
questions.additionalInfo: The Bonnethead Shark is the only omnivorous shark, feeding on both animal and plant matter, including seagrass.

questions.question: how does the Chain Catshark signal to other members of its species?
answers: By jumping out of the water
questions.additionalInfo: The Chain Catshark has bioluminescent organs that allow it to glow green, which it uses to signal to other members of its species.
